message:
release: drop the @textsift scope I invented; trim umbrella tarball

Two release-prep cleanups:

1) Per-triple subpackage names: rename `@textsift/native-<triple>`
   → `textsift-native-<triple>` (unscoped) across publish-natives.mjs,
   src/native/backend.ts (runtime resolution), README, HANDOFF.md,
   and the .gitignore comment.

   The `@textsift` org doesn't exist on npm and there's no plan to
   create one. Everything publishes from a single personal account
   under unscoped names. Umbrella stays `textsift`.

2) Umbrella package files: switch the `files` field from `dist/`
   (everything) to an explicit allowlist. Drops the duplicate
   standalone `.wasm` files (already inlined into index.js per the
   turboquant-wasm pattern), the locally-built `.node` (comes from
   the per-triple subpackage at install time), and the `.map`
   sourcemaps. Tarball goes from 1.3 MB → 376 KB packed, 16.6 MB
   → 5.2 MB unpacked, 79 → 39 files.

files.patch:
@@ -30,6 +30,6 @@ packages/textsift/vendor/dawn/lib/*.dll
 # across OS by scripts/bench-aggregate.mjs into the workflow summary).
 bench-results.json
 # Per-triple subpackage staging dir written by scripts/publish-natives.mjs.
-# Contents are published to npm as @textsift/native-{linux-x64,...} and
+# Contents are published to npm as textsift-native-{linux-x64,...} and
 # linked from the umbrella package's optionalDependencies.
 dist-pkg/

@@ -51,7 +51,7 @@ End-to-end on a 122-character input with 4 PII spans: `redact()` returns in ~50
 | Windows x86_64 | Dawn-direct | Tint → D3D12 via statically-linked Google Dawn |
 | (any platform, no GPU) | WASM fallback | Zig + SIMD128 in WebAssembly |
 
-Each platform's `.node` binary is built with comptime-gated Zig code so it only contains the relevant backend — Mac binaries don't ship Vulkan code, Windows binaries don't ship Obj-C, etc. npm picks the right `optionalDependencies` subpackage at install time (`@textsift/native-{linux-x64,linux-arm64,darwin-x64,darwin-arm64,win32-x64}`).
+Each platform's `.node` binary is built with comptime-gated Zig code so it only contains the relevant backend — Mac binaries don't ship Vulkan code, Windows binaries don't ship Obj-C, etc. npm picks the right `optionalDependencies` subpackage at install time (`textsift-native-{linux-x64,linux-arm64,darwin-x64,darwin-arm64,win32-x64}`).
 
 ## Linux prereqs (one-time)

@@ -124,7 +124,7 @@ GitHub Actions matrix + `optionalDependencies` are committed at `.github/workflo
 
 When the repo is pushed and the matrix runs for the first time, expect ~30–60 min for the cold Dawn build per OS; subsequent runs hit the per-OS cache and complete in 10–20 s.
 
-`@textsift/native-{darwin-arm64,darwin-x64,linux-x64,linux-arm64,win32-x64}` per-triple subpackages are wired through `release.yml`. NPM_TOKEN secret needs to be set in repo settings before `release.yml` works.
+`textsift-native-{darwin-arm64,darwin-x64,linux-x64,linux-arm64,win32-x64}` per-triple subpackages are wired through `release.yml`. NPM_TOKEN secret needs to be set in repo settings before `release.yml` works.
 
 ### PrivacyFilter wiring (DONE)
